allOf.comment: Display label ('MasterAuto'). Set from GVL_HMI init, never written by FB.

HMI writes -- per-step Simulate flags. Rising edge shortcuts the current
 step's wait condition (FB clears the flag on consumption via R_TRIG.Q).
HMI writes -- momentary command buttons (R_TRIG inside FB latches the rising edge)
FB writes -- status
0 = OK; see FB_MasterAutoCycle header for the code table
incremented at end of SEP_RETRACTED_DWELL
human-readable mirror of eStep
seconds spent in the current step (0.0 on transition)

If TRUE, SEP_RETRACTED_DWELL returns to IDLE and immediately re-arms;
 otherwise it goes to IDLE and waits for the next Start / bAtPos1.
DWELL_PUSH -- hold at push-extended
PUSH_RETRACTED_DWELL -- hold after push retract
SEP_RETRACTED_DWELL -- hold after sep retract before IDLE

Timeout guards every non-DWELL waiting state. On expiry, iErrorCode is
 set to the step's numeric code and the cycle enters ERR.

How many piston pairs participate in the cycle (1..3). All enabled pairs
 move in parallel; the FB waits for AND of all enabled sensors before
 advancing. Clamped 1..3 by the FB at read time.
Display label ("Piston 1", "Ejector", ...). Set from GVL_HMI, never written by FB.
HMI reads (FB writes) — effective sensor = real OR simulate
HMI writes — latched command targets (momentary press, rising edge selects)
HMI writes — jog while held (TRUE while operator holds the JOG button)
to be able to control from the VISU old HMI
HMI reads — physical solenoid coil states (FB mirrors them every cycle)
HMI reads (FB writes) — latched-target highlight
HMI writes — simulate sensor inputs (OR-merged with real). Future Sim checkbox hook.
HMI writes — mode
HMI writes — momentary commands (R_TRIG inside FB latches the rising edge)
FB writes — status (never touched by HMI)
incremented at end of each DWELL_RET
human-readable mirror of eStep
seconds spent in the current step (0.0 on transition)
If TRUE, DWELL_RET loops back to EXTENDING instead of returning to IDLE.

All times in milliseconds. UDINT so HMI NumericInput binds directly (no TIME<->UDINT juggling).
 Dwell = how long to sit at the sensor before starting the reverse move.
Timeout = if we do not see the target sensor within this window, raise an error.
Display label ('Robot'). Set from GVL_Robot, never written by bridge.

Bridge writes — latched "in position N" flags. Bridge holds the last-seen
 position TRUE and clears the other two when a new POS frame arrives.

Inbound pulse — bridge writes TRUE on receiving a RESET_ERROR frame.
 FB_MasterAutoCycle consumes on rising edge and clears the flag.

Outbound pulses — PLC (FB_MasterAutoCycle) writes TRUE for one cycle.
 Bridge polls each on rising edge, sends the corresponding TCP frame,
 then clears the flag. If the bridge is disconnected the flag stays
 TRUE until reconnect; no queue, no lost pulses.
Bridge writes — connection state (enum + string mirror for HMI text binding)
Bridge writes — diagnostics
Bridge writes — outbound diagnostics

FILESREGEX.FILESHTTPHEADERS:
Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; connect-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; manifest-src 'self'; form-action 'self'; font-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'
 Content-Type: text/html

Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'none'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; frame-ancestors 'self'
 Content-Type: image/svg+xml

allOf.description: Execute the request for a different ADS port

allOf.comment:
Runtime state for the master (multi-piston + robot-coordinated) auto
 sequence. Config for it lives in GVL_HmiPersistent.stMasterAutoCfg.

Runtime state for the PushSol1 auto sequencer. Config for it lives in
 GVL_HmiPersistent.stPushSol1AutoCfg (PERSISTENT).

Runtime state for the SepSol1 auto sequencer. Config for it lives in
 GVL_HmiPersistent.stSepSol1AutoCfg (PERSISTENT).

Config for the master (multi-piston + robot-coordinated) auto sequence.
 See ST_HmiMasterAutoCfg for the tunable list; defaults land on cold-boot.

Auto-cycle configuration. PERSISTENT so operator-tuned dwell / timeout
 values survive an Activate Configuration + PLC restart. Values reset
 to the struct defaults only on a full cold-boot reset.

Runtime toggle for DEBUG severity. F_LogEvent drops DBG entries
 when this is FALSE. Operator can flip it from the HMI without a
 rebuild. HMI ToggleSwitch on the Robot page binds here.

domain.description: Domain name of the authentication extension

anyOf.description: Parameters that are forwarded to the authentication extension's AddUser function. Notice that the userName without the domain is forwarded automatically.

settings.description: Changes to the USERGROUPUSERS entry of the user

userName.description: Name of the user without domain prefix

AUTOMAP.description: Automatically obtain symbol information such as options and schema from the domain.

CHECKCOMPLEXITY.description: Do not add symbol, check complexity of added symbol instead

anyOf.description: Changes to the USERGROUPUSERS entry of the current user.

allOf.description: Parameters that are forwarded to the authentication extension's ChangePassword function.
Calling this method without writeValue will read the current settings.

fileName.description: Name of the file to delete.

anyOf.description: User name. The authentication domain can be specified by using 'Domain::UserName'. If no domain is specified all users with the given name will be logged out.

clientCertificate.description: Thumbprint of the used client certificate

mergeSettings.description: Merge settings from the symbol configuration into the schema.

authExtension.description: Shows if extension can be used for user management.

configVersion.description: The version of the extension's configuration.

debuggerAttached.description: Indicates whether a debugger is or will be attached to the extension.

friendlyName.description: A more readable name version of the extension name.

guid.description: The globally unique identifier associated with the extension.

licensed.description: Indicate the extension is license status.

packageVersion.description: The version of the NuGet package that contains the extension.

remote.description: Shows if the domain refers to a remote server.

required.description: Shows if the extension is considered to be required.

state.description: Shows the current state of the extension.

thirdParty.description: Indicate the extension is a customer extension.

updated.description: Shows last update time of the config.

visibility.description: Indicates under which circumstances the extension configuration should be visible.

hash.description: The md5 hash of the file

hashes.description: If true, the hash of each file is returned.

path.description: The path to list files from.

recursive.description: If true, the entire directory tree is returned.

items.description: List of all users and their permissions.
List of all users and their permissions.

SCHEMA.description: The SessionId of the logged in user.

domain.description: Domain name of the authentication extension

userName.description: Name of the user without domain prefix

enabled.description: Indicates if the user can be used or not.

password.description: Password of the user. Only a hash will be stored.

newUserName.description: The new name of the user.

checkSum.description: Checksum of the file as MD5 hexadecimal-string.

domain.description: Upload extension dll to remote filesystem.
